description: Wishmax Landing Page (Marketing Website) - Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind marketing site that communicates the hook, proves results with real before/after transformations, and converts visitors into waitlist signups (swappable to App Store install at launch). Consumes PRD-01 design tokens.

Brand fonts loaded and applied

userStories.notes: Done 2026-07-01. Next.js 16 (App Router, Turbopack) + React 19 + TS + Tailwind v4 scaffolded at web/. globals.css @imports ../../design/fonts.css + ../../design/tokens.css (single source of truth, no token copies); Turbopack root set to repo root so the parent-dir imports resolve. Browser-verified: white canvas, Space Grotesk 48px black H1, black CTA (backgroundImage:none), no boilerplate.

Header: Wishmax wordmark (Inter 700, not Space Grotesk <26px) + desktop nav (How it works / Examples / FAQ) + black 'Get my photos' CTA; collapses to a hamburger-toggled stacked menu ≤767px. userStories.notes: Done 2026-07-01. Hero (web/app/components/Hero.tsx + hero.css) leads with the Space Grotesk hook headline (36px→48px), eyebrow Badge, subhead naming the pain (your photos are costing you matches) + promise (pro dating photos from your own face, no photoshoot), above-the-fold black CTA (Get my photos → #waitlist) + ghost CTA (See the transformations → #examples), and the PRD-01 Before/After card as proof. Ported Badge + FeedPhotoTile (rewired to next/image fill + priority/sizes, non-interactive) + BeforeAfterCard into web/app/components/ (+ showcase.css slice). Real photographic-register raster stand-ins in web/public/hero/ generated by web/scripts/gen-hero-images.mjs (sharp SVG→JPG; swappable to licensed photos at same paths, no code change) — NOT gradient placeholders; honest 'Sample transformation' caption. Mobile-first single column → 2-col at ≥768px on the periwinkle wash surface. No CLS (fixed 3:4 aspect-ratio); LCP images priority → <link rel=preload as=image> for both tiles in SSR. Upload a few selfies, 2. Pick photos you love, 3. Section on the white canvas (box-shadow none — elevation is cards-only). Typecheck, lint, build all pass; token-literal audit CLEAN. Browser-verified 390/768/1280 (single col → 3 col, white section no shadow, cards --shadow-sm/12px, 3 icons+badges+titles+sentences).

userStories.notes: Done 2026-07-01. Gallery section (web/app/components/Gallery.tsx + gallery.css, rendered by page.tsx after HowItWorks) with id='examples' matching the header/footer 'Examples' nav anchor + the hero's 'See the transformations' CTA. Eight curated sample result tiles rendered with the ported PRD-01 FeedPhotoTile (THE app feed component → gallery mirrors the app's feel), each with a category chip (Travel/Gym/Professional/Night out ×2 via the tile's category prop → Badge onPhoto over the scrim) to show range. next/image + lazy-load (no priority → all 8 emit loading='lazy', verified none preloaded in SSR); fixed 3:4 aspect-ratio → no CLS/jank (verified aspect 1.333). Real photographic-register raster stand-ins in web/public/gallery/ generated by web/scripts/gen-gallery-images.mjs (sharp SVG→JPG, one palette+lighting per category, varied people; reuses the gen-hero-images.mjs pattern; swappable to licensed photos at same paths, no code change) — NOT gradient placeholders; honest 'Sample transformations' caption. userStories.notes: Done 2026-07-01. Benefits section (web/app/components/Benefits.tsx + benefits.css, rendered by page.tsx after Gallery) with id='benefits'. Maps FOUR concrete, named pain points → Wishmax outcomes, each benefit → exactly one pain: Bad lighting → 'Studio-grade light on every shot'; No good photos → 'A full lineup of scroll-stopping shots'; Expensive photographers → 'Pro results without the pro price tag'; Low matches → 'Photos that pull more right-swipes'. Each is a DESIGN.md Standard Content Card (white surface, --shadow-sm, radius-cards 12px) with a filled mono-weight inline-SVG icon (sun/gallery/tag/heart, currentColor ink, 20px) on a soft ink-tint chip, the pain named as a strong-tone Badge, an Inter-700 outcome title, and ONE tight sentence of specific, confident brand-voice proof copy (no lorem ipsum) — two free-text sizes/card (title+body), pain rides on the Badge → max-two-type honored. Reuses the established eyebrow-Badge + Inter-700-24px-title + slate-lede section header and the HowItWorks icon-chip pattern. Section on the white canvas (rhythm against the Gallery periwinkle wash), box-shadow none. Grid 1 → 2 → 2 cols (4 benefits as a 2×2). Typecheck, lint, build all pass; token-literal audit CLEAN. Browser-verified 390/768/1280 (white section no shadow, Inter-700 24px title, 4 cards each with icon+pain-Badge+outcome-title+sentence, cards --shadow-sm/12px, grid 1→2→2).

Three testimonial cards as DESIGN.md Standard Content Cards (white surface, --shadow-sm, radius-cards 12px), each with: a 5-star rating in filled mono-weight INK glyphs (black/white action system — no gold/colored accent; role=img + aria-label 'Rated 5 out of 5'), a brand-voice quote (no lorem ipsum), and a credible attribution row — a real-photography avatar via next/image (circular: border-radius 960px + overflow hidden, distinct faces), a name (Jordan M./Devin R./Marcus T.) and a handle. Two text sizes/card (quote=heading-sm + caption-size name/handle; stars are icons) → max-two-type honored. Avatars are self-authored studio-portrait raster stand-ins in web/public/testimonials/ generated by web/scripts/gen-avatar-images.mjs (reuses the gen-hero/gen-gallery portrait pattern, square 1:1, varied people), swappable to licensed photos at the same paths with zero code change — NOT generic stock; honest 'Illustrative pre-launch previews — real member stories replace these at launch' caption (content structure ready for real quotes/metrics). Lazy-loaded (no priority → loading='lazy', verified NOT preloaded in SSR). Section on the periwinkle→white wash (surface only) to continue the alternating rhythm (Benefits above is white); box-shadow none; title Inter 700 24px (not the display face); reuses the eyebrow-Badge + Inter-700-title + slate-lede header. Grid 1 → 3 → 3 cols. Typecheck, lint, build all pass; token-literal audit CLEAN. Browser-verified 390/768/1280 (periwinkle wash no section shadow, 3 cards w/ 5 ink stars + quote + circular 40×40 avatar + name + handle, cards --shadow-sm/12px, grid 1→3→3, avatars lazy).

userStories.title: FAQ section
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want common objections answered so I feel safe converting.
acceptanceCriteria: Accessible accordion FAQ covering: is it really my face, photo privacy/data handling, how long it takes, platforms (iOS/Android), and cost/cancellation
Keyboard accessible and responsive
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Done 2026-07-01. FAQ section (web/app/components/Faq.tsx + faq.css, rendered by page.tsx after SocialProof, id='faq' matching the header/footer 'FAQ' nav anchor). Accessible accordion following the WAI-ARIA pattern: each question is an <h3><button aria-expanded aria-controls>; each answer is a role='region' aria-labelledby panel that is `hidden` while collapsed (leaves the tab order + skipped by AT). Five items cover ALL required objections: 'Is it really my face?', 'What happens to my photos and data?' (privacy/data handling), 'How long does it take?', 'What platforms is Wishmax on?' (iOS/Android), 'What does it cost, and can I cancel?' (cost/cancellation). Keyboard accessible via native <button> triggers (Tab focuses, Enter opens, Space closes — verified aria-expanded + panel hidden flip on both keys); items open independently (Set<number> state in a 'use client' component, useId for stable ids). Copy is brand-voice, honest pre-launch (pricing/platform timing framed 'at launch'), no lorem ipsum. Each item is a DESIGN.md Standard Content Card (white surface, --shadow-sm, radius-cards 12px) with a full-width trigger and a filled mono INK chevron that rotates 180deg on open; section on the white canvas (no gradient, box-shadow none) for rhythm against the SocialProof periwinkle wash above; single readable column (max 72ch) at every width; title Inter 700 24px (not the display face); reuses the eyebrow-Badge + Inter-700-title + slate-lede section header. Typecheck, lint, build all pass; token-literal audit CLEAN. Browser-verified 390/768/1280 (white section no shadow, Inter 24px title, 5 panels start hidden, aria-controls/aria-labelledby wired, click+Enter open / Space close, independent open, cards --shadow-sm/12px).

userStories.title: Primary CTA + waitlist email capture
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want one obvious next step so I can install or get notified.
acceptanceCriteria: Pre-launch, the primary CTA captures an email for the waitlist; the CTA target is structured to later swap to an App Store / TestFlight link without re-architecting

Waitlist submissions persist via a Next.js route handler behind a thin store abstraction (single saveWaitlistEmail seam); v1 may use a documented placeholder store swappable to the real provider without touching callers
Validation and success/error states handled
No secrets exposed client-side; submission has a concrete spam guard (per-IP rate limit on the route handler and/or hidden honeypot field), verified to reject rapid/bot submissions
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Done 2026-07-01. Waitlist section (web/app/components/Waitlist.tsx + waitlist.css, rendered by page.tsx after Faq, id='waitlist' matching the header/hero CTA anchors) captures a pre-launch email. CTA SWAP SEAM: primary CTA target centralized in web/app/lib/cta.ts (PRIMARY_CTA {mode:'waitlist',href:'#waitlist',label}); SiteHeader + Hero now consume it → flipping mode to 'store' + an App Store/TestFlight href is a one-line change, no component re-architecting. STORE SEAM: POST /api/waitlist route handler (web/app/api/waitlist/route.ts, runtime nodejs) persists behind a single saveWaitlistEmail(entry) seam (web/app/lib/waitlist-store.ts) — v1 placeholder appends JSONL to a gitignored web/.waitlist-data/waitlist.jsonl, documented and swappable to a real provider (Resend/ConvertKit/DB) by replacing only that function body. SPAM GUARDS (both): hidden honeypot field ('company', HONEYPOT_FIELD const shared client+server) → filled = bot → returns 200 but NEVER persists; AND per-IP fixed-window rate limit (web/app/lib/rate-limit.ts, 5/min/IP via x-forwarded-for) → 6th rapid req → 429 + Retry-After. Validation (shared isValidEmail in web/app/lib/waitlist.ts, used client-side for instant feedback + re-checked authoritatively on the server): invalid → 400 inline error; malformed JSON → 400 bad_request. States: idle→submitting(Button loading)→success(form replaced by an ink-check confirmation card)|error(inline message, aria-invalid on input). No secrets client-side (store is server-only node:fs; route imports it, form only fetches). a11y: sr-only label, aria-describedby feedback, role=status aria-live. Section on periwinkle wash (rhythm vs FAQ white above); title Inter 700 24px; input 2px midnight-ink focus ring + ink error border (black/white system — form errors NOT red, status colors reserved for generation). Typecheck, lint, build all pass; token-literal audit CLEAN. API verified via curl (valid 200+persisted / invalid 400 / honeypot 200-not-stored / malformed 400 / 6th req 429). Browser-verified 390/768/1280 (periwinkle wash no section shadow, Inter 24px title, honeypot tabindex=-1 aria-hidden clipped, input radius 8px + 2px black focus ring, invalid→error+aria-invalid, valid→success card replaces form).
